朝.origin: Grass and sun on the left, moon 月 on the right — the sun rises while the moon fades, that's morning.

sentence.en: The morning sun is called the rising sun.

清.origin: Water 氵 plus 青 (blue-green) — clear, clean water you can almost see right through.

sentence.en: The water in the little river is very clear.

皇.origin: A bright crown 白 sitting on top of a king 王 — the grand emperor of all.

sentence.en: Long ago there was an emperor.

位.origin: A person 亻 standing 立 in one spot — the place or seat where someone belongs.

sentence.en: Please go back to your seat.

帝.origin: Picture a tall altar where offerings were made — the ruler above everyone is the emperor 帝.

sentence.en: The emperor lives in a big palace.

居.origin: A door 户 above 古 (old) — where you settle down behind your door is where you live.

sentence.en: We live in the city.

殿.origin: A grand, sturdy building where kings sat — think of a great royal hall 殿.

sentence.en: This palace is really beautiful.

间.origin: The sun 日 peeking through a doorway 门 — the space in between, like a room.

sentence.en: My room is very clean.

筑.origin: Bamboo ⺮ over 巩 (strong) — strong bamboo poles used to build things up.

sentence.en: This building is very tall.

藏.origin: Grass 艹 on top hiding something underneath — slip away and conceal yourself in the grass.

sentence.en: The kitten is hiding under the table.

内.origin: A person 人 stepping through a door frame 冂 — going inside, the inner part.

sentence.en: It is very warm inside the house.

颐.origin: A head 页 on the right — 颐 means the cheeks and jaw, the soft part of your face.

sentence.en: We went to play at the Summer Palace.

久.origin: A simple stroke like a person walking on and on — 久 means a long, long time.

sentence.en: We haven't seen each other for a long time.

站.origin: Stand 立 next to 占 — to stand up straight, or a station where buses stop.

sentence.en: I wait for mom at the bus station.

底.origin: A shelter 广 over 氐 (low) — the lowest, bottom part underneath.

sentence.en: The ball rolled under the table.

岛.origin: A bird 鸟 resting on a mountain 山 poking out of the sea — that's an island.

sentence.en: There is a small island in the sea.

桥.origin: Wood 木 plus 乔 (tall) — tall wooden planks laid across the water make a bridge.

sentence.en: Water flows under the little bridge.

洞.origin: Water 氵 plus 同 — water hollows out the rock to make a deep cave or hole.

sentence.en: The little bear lives in a cave.

孔.origin: A baby 子 beside a little curl 乚 — picture the tiny mouth of a crying baby, a small hole.

sentence.en: There is a small hole in the needle.

柱.origin: Wood 木 plus 主 (main) — a main wooden post that holds the roof up, a pillar.

sentence.en: There are four pillars in the hall.

狮.origin: The animal sign 犭 plus 师 for the sound — the big, brave lion king.

sentence.en: The lion is the king of the grassland.

分.origin: A knife 刀 cutting something into pieces 八 — to split or divide things up.

sentence.en: I divided the cake among everyone.

廊.origin: A wide roof 广 with 郎 for the sound — a long covered walkway, a corridor.

sentence.en: There is a corridor outside the classroom.

眼.origin: An eye 目 plus 艮 — your eyes, the little windows you see the world through.

sentence.en: Her eyes are big and bright.

寿.origin: An older form of 老 (old) — 寿 means a long life and growing happily old.

sentence.en: Wishing grandpa health and a long life.

骆.origin: A horse 马 plus 各 for the sound — but with a hump on its back, it's a camel.

sentence.en: The camel walks in the desert.

驼.origin: A horse 马 plus 它 — a humped desert horse, the camel that carries loads.

sentence.en: Camels can carry many things on their backs.

件.origin: A person 亻 beside a cow 牛 — dividing things up, so 件 counts items one by one.

sentence.en: I bought a new piece of clothing.

俩.origin: A person 亻 plus 两 (two) — 俩 simply means the two of them, a pair.

sentence.en: The two of us are good friends.

扒.origin: A hand 扌 plus 八 — using your hand to scratch, push aside, or dig something up.

sentence.en: The puppy digs the soil with its paws.

肯.origin: Stop 止 above flesh ⺼ — when you stop and settle, you agree and say yes.

sentence.en: He is willing to help me with homework.

输.origin: A cart 车 plus 俞 — carts haul and carry things; 输 also means to lose a game.

sentence.en: We lost the game this time.

窄.origin: A cave 穴 over 乍 — a tiny squeezed opening, so 窄 means narrow and tight.

sentence.en: This little path is very narrow.

跪.origin: A foot 足 plus 危 — bending your legs all the way down to kneel.

sentence.en: He knelt on the ground to look for the key.

钻.origin: Metal 钅 plus 占 — a hard metal tool that bores holes; 钻石 is a sparkling diamond.

sentence.en: This diamond is very shiny.

找.origin: A hand 扌 holding a spear 戈 — searching all around, hunting for what you want to find.

sentence.en: I found my book.

评.origin: Speech 讠 plus 平 (level) — using fair words to judge and comment on things.

sentence.en: The teacher graded my essay.

短.origin: A dart 矢 next to 豆 — a tiny arrow is little, so 短 means short and brief.

sentence.en: My pencil is too short.

伯.origin: A person 亻 plus 白 (white) — picture an older man with white hair, your uncle 伯.

sentence.en: Uncle gave me a book as a gift.

深.origin: Water 氵 plus 罙 — water that goes way, way down; 深 means deep.

sentence.en: This pool is very deep.
